summary: Iteration 2 testing completed. Backend 100% pass (18/18 tests). Found and fixed 2 critical bugs: Accelerometer web compatibility and useFocusEffect import. All 4 new features verified on dashboard. 3 claimed bug fixes partially verified due to navigation complexity.

react_native_issues.issue: Accelerometer.addListener() fails on web - _nativeModule undefined. FIXED by wrapping in try/catch with platform check
useFocusEffect imported from @react-navigation/native instead of expo-router. Causes NavigationContainer error when navigating directly to routes. FIXED by changing import source

action_items: Verify useFocusEffect fix works correctly after Metro cache clears (may need app restart)
Test automations tab navigation thoroughly to confirm 3 automations load via useFocusEffect
Test complete create automation flow with all trigger types
Verify AI chat can handle multiple message exchanges
Test all filter buttons in Journal tab

success_rate.frontend: 80% (dashboard + new features verified, tab navigation partially tested due to useFocusEffect bug found and fixed)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Backend already seeded with 3 automations and 2 knowledge entries via /api/seed endpoint
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two critical bugs found and fixed during testing: (1) Accelerometer fails on web - added try/catch wrapper, (2) useFocusEffect imported from wrong package (@react-navigation/native instead of expo-router) causing NavigationContainer errors. After fixes, Metro cache may need full clear (restart Expo). All backend tests passing. Dashboard loads with all 4 new features. Claimed bug fixes from iteration 1 verified in code but runtime verification incomplete due to navigation issues.
rca_of_issue:
ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:

1. ACCELEROMETER CRASH (CRITICAL):
   - Root Cause: expo-sensors Accelerometer.addListener() assumes native module exists. On web preview, _nativeModule is undefined.
   - Impact: Dashboard screen crashed immediately on load, blocking all testing.
   - Fix Applied: Wrapped Accelerometer setup in try/catch with platform check in index.tsx lines 43-60.
   - Verification: Dashboard now loads successfully on web preview.

2. USEFOCUSEFFECT NAVIGATION ERROR (CRITICAL):
   - Root Cause: useFocusEffect imported from @react-navigation/native but app uses Expo Router which has its own navigation context.
   - Impact: Direct navigation to /automations or /journal routes crashed with 'NavigationContainer not found' error.
   - Affected Files: automations.tsx line 8, journal.tsx line 8
   - Fix Applied: Changed import from '@react-navigation/native' to 'expo-router' in both files.
   - Verification: Needs retest after Metro cache clears, but fix is correct per Expo Router documentation.

3. ITERATION 1 BUG FIXES STATUS:
   - BUG FIX 1 (useFocusEffect): Implementation correct in code, but blocked by import issue (now fixed).
   - BUG FIX 2 (duplicate testID): Verified resolved - learn-btn-assistant and learn-btn-settings exist separately.
   - BUG FIX 3 (create button): Verified in code at line 116 automations.tsx with correct testID.

4. NEW FEATURES STATUS:
   - All 4 new features (resource mode banner, screen capture card, sensors card, device tags) verified visible on dashboard.
   - Sensors show 0,0,0 on web (expected - accelerometer not supported in browser).
   - Resource mode shows 'Normal' with 'Libre' (unrestricted) badge correctly.
